user_query:
I need an automated pass over my field notes to extract story sections and quotes, anonymize storyteller names, and update my anonymization tracking. Please implement a small local workflow that does the following using only the files in this workspace:

Inputs
- Read all Markdown notes under input/notes/ (e.g., input/notes/day1.md, input/notes/day2.md).
- Use input/roster.csv as the canonical map from real names to pseudonyms.
- Use config/anonymizer.yaml for clan/affiliation terms to ignore and to record any storyteller names that still need pseudonyms.

Requirements
1) Story and quote extraction
- Identify stories by headings that start with either "### Story:" or "### Origin Story:".
- For each story, read the immediately following line that begins with "Storyteller:" and parse the storyteller name. When parsing the name:
  - Remove anything in parentheses and any trailing clan/affiliation phrases that exactly match items in config/anonymizer.yaml under ignore_clan_terms.
  - Trim whitespace and preserve any diacritics in the canonical storyteller name you extract from the notes.
- Extract direct quotes from the story body as any text enclosed in straight double quotation marks ("). Ignore any quotes that are outside the story section.

2) Anonymization
- Map storyteller names to pseudonyms using input/roster.csv with case- and accent-insensitive matching (e.g., map "Anaï" to the roster name "Anai" and "Rūko" to "Ruko").
- If a storyteller name from the notes does not have a match in input/roster.csv after applying case- and accent-insensitive comparison, treat it as unknown: use the literal value "PENDING" for storyteller_pseudonym in outputs and append the canonical storyteller name (as it appears in the notes, preserving diacritics) to the pending_names list in config/anonymizer.yaml. Ensure pending_names contains unique values and is sorted case-insensitively after the update.

3) Outputs (create directories as needed and overwrite if they exist)
- Write out/story_index.csv with columns: note_file (relative path), story_title (text after the heading prefix), storyteller_pseudonym (from roster.csv or "PENDING"), quote_count (integer number of extracted quotes in that story).
- Write out/quotes.jsonl where each line is a JSON object with keys: note_file, story_title, storyteller_pseudonym (from roster.csv or "PENDING"), and quote_text.
- Modify config/anonymizer.yaml in place to update the pending_names list as described.
- Draft an email to my colleague Maya saved as out/email_to_maya.txt. Start the file with:
  To: maya@example.com
  Subject: Extracted stories and anonymization status
  Then include a short body that:
  - States the total number of stories found and the total number of quotes extracted.
  - Lists the names added to pending_names (if any) as a simple list.
  - Mentions the paths to out/story_index.csv and out/quotes.jsonl.
  - Includes 1–2 sentences on how this helps us coordinate on anonymization for her research.

Constraints
- Do not rely on any external resources or network calls; everything must be derived from the provided files.
- Accent-insensitive matching is required for name-to-pseudonym mapping.
- Only modify config/anonymizer.yaml by updating the pending_names list; keep other keys intact.

Files to use are in this repository. Input paths are fixed, and outputs must be written exactly to the specified out/ paths and the existing config path. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
